Discover the top **20 reasons to use skills assessment tests**. Skill assessment tests are used to evaluate both your current and potential employees. Using skill assessment tests is essential if you want to hire the best candidates and develop top performers.

## Why do companies use skill assessment test?

**Skill assessment tests** are tests designed to help employers **evaluate the knowledge and the skills** of their job **candidates**, as well as their current **employees**.

Using a skill assessment test helps companies to ensure that their job candidates, as well as their current employees, **have the** **required skills** to successfully perform their jobs.

## Importance of skill assessment tests

Skills assessments tests have **been around** for **decades**. While companies were **initially** **slow** to adopt the use of skill assessment test, they have become a **rising** **recruitment trend** in **recent** years.

➡️ If you want to learn more about the **latest recruiting trends**, download free eBook: **15 Recruiting Trends You Should Implement in 2019** !

Thanks to the rapid **scientific and** **technological advancement** , skill assessment test have become a **reliable, affordable and user-friendly** way for companies to test their employees and candidates.

These days, **82% of companies are using some form of skill assessment tests**, according to the Talent Board’s Candidate Experience Research report.

Skill-assessment-tests-prevalence

Employer use skill assessment test in **two main ways:**

1. To test job **candidates**
2. To test their existing **employees**.

In the following text, we will carefully examine the **reasons** for administering skill assessment tests on both of these two groups.

## Using skill assessment tests to evaluate candidates

Skill assessments test are often used by **employers** to evaluate job **candidates** skills and knowledge.

Skill assessment test used to test job candidates’ skills and knowledge are sometimes also called **pre-employment, pre-hiring or candidate assessment tests.**

Asking job candidates to take a skill assessment test is a **common** step in **the recruitment process** which helps employers **choose and hire** the best candidates.

There are many **advantages** of using skill assessment tests along with the **traditional** selection methods such as reviewing resumes and **conducting job interviews.**

Here are the **key reasons** why employer use **skill assessment tests** to **evaluate** job candidates:

### Reason #1: To test candidates’ claims

According to Criteria Pre-Employment Testing, up to **78% of resumes are misleading** and up to 46% contain actual lies!

Skill assessment test help employers **check the truthfulness of candidates’ resumes** .

By applying skill assessments test, employers can verify is their candidates **really** possess the skills they **claim** to have.

Reasons-to-use-skill-assessment-test-candidates-lie-in-their-resumes

### Reason #2: Determine a job fit

Applying a skill assessment test are one of the best ways to determine whether a candidate is a **good match for your job position** you are looking to fill.

Skill assessments tests can provide relevant information on a job candidate’s **ability to perform well** in the job.

### Reason #3: Determine a cultural fit

By using skill assessment test employers can determine candidates’ **cultural fit** more effectively.

Your candidates can have the best **technical** skills, but if they don’t have the necessary **communication** skills to fit in your existing **team**, they are sure to **fail** and probably even **leave** your company!

Skill-assessment-test-determine-cultural-fit

### Reason #4: To avoid making a bad hire

According to Career Builder research, **74% of employers** admit they’ve **hired the wrong person** for a position. ‍♀️

Skill-assessment-test-avoid-bad-hire

Skills assessment tests help employers **check if their perfect candidates are really a great fit** for the job and the company culture as they claim to be.

As a result, making a **bad hire** is much **less common** among employers who use skill assessment tests.

### Reason #5: To handle high volume hiring

Skill assessment test are especially useful for handling **high volume hiring.** When you have a **large number of applicants**, using a skill assessment test as a **pre-screening method** can help you **differentiate** the top candidates **early** in the hiring process.

As a result, you can **avoid wasting a ton of time** interviewing unqualified candidates.

### Reason #6: To differentiate between similar candidates

Skill assessment test are also a great solution for **differentiating** between **similar** candidates.

Skill assessment test can provide valuable insights and **capture the nuances** between candidates who seems to have the **same** education and experience.

### Reason #7: To eliminate bias

Using a skill assessment test is one of the best ways to **eliminate bias in recruitment** .

Reason-zo-use.skill-assessment-test-eliminate bias

Skill assessment test provide an accurate, **evidence-based** information on candidates’ skills and knowledge.

Unlike **resumes and interviews** which necessarily provide more **detailed** information on candidates which can be **judged** (even **unconsciously**!) by recruiters, skill assessment tests provide a **more objective evaluation** of candidates’ skills.

### Reason #8: To minimize legal risks

Skill assessment test are also your best bet for **avoiding legal risks of hiring**.

Since skill assessments tests are by their definition **standardized** and **objective** way to evaluate candidates, they can help your company **defend** your choice of the candidate you hired in a potential law suite.

### Reason #9: To improve the candidate experience

Skill assessment tests can also significantly **improve the candidate experience** with your company.

Today’s candidates want **brief, informative, and engaging** hiring experience. They also want to know they were given a **fair chance** to compete for your open job position.

By using skill assessment tests, you can provide a **timely** and **well argued** explanation of your hiring decisions.

### Reason #10: To enhance employer brand

Skill assessment test can also be used to **enhance your company’s employer brand** .

Companies that **present** themselves as having **highly talented employees** need to have a process in place that **supports** that assertion.

Using skill assessment tests can **reinforce** that image make the candidates who **pass** your assessments feel that they have joined an **exclusive** group.

### Reason #11: To address the skills gap

Using skills assessment tests is an effective method to address the **serious problem of skills gaps** among job candidates.

Recent research conducted by the Society for Human Resource Management has found that **75% of HR professionals** who reported difficulty recruiting in the past 12 months say there are **skills gaps** in job candidates.

Skill assessment test can provide **valuable insights** into the ability of candidates to **learn new things and upgrade** their existing skills.

### Reason #12: To reduce turnover

Research conducted by the Aberdeen Group has found that companies who use pre-hire assessment tests report a **39% lower turnover rate.**

### Reason #13: To save time

By using the skill assessment test early in the hiring process, companies can **eliminate unqualified** candidates **quickly** and easily.

That way, they can ensure that **only** the most **suitable** candidates progress through the selection stages and that hiring managers’ interview **time** is **focused** only on the best candidates.

### Reason #14: To save money

Using a skill assessment test can help you save money in **two key ways.**

First, skill assessment test can **eliminate unqualified** candidates **early** in the selection process, thus **saving** your hiring managers’ **time**. **Shorter** and more effective hiring process equals **less money spent.**

Another way using skill assessment test saves your company’s money is by **avoiding the high cost of a bad hire** and hiring only the best, **most productive** candidates. According to Career Builder, the average cost of a single bad hire is **nearly $15,000.**

### Reason #15: To ensure higher hiring managers satisfaction

Hiring managers who utilize pre-employment assessments report 36% more satisfaction with their final decision than those who don’t, according to Aberdeen Group study.

### Reason #16: To ensure high employee performance

Aberdeen Group study has also found that organizations who use pre-hire assessments are **24% more likely** to have employees who **exceed performance goals.**

## Using skill assessment tests to evaluate employees

Using a skill assessment test for evaluating a company’s **existing** employees is known as a **talent assessment.**

Talent assessment is a very **useful** method which managers use to ensure that they’ve got the **right** people in the **right** job positions.

If used correctly, talent assessments can make a **profound** impact on employee **performance** and **productivity** and do wonders for your company’s bottom line.

Here are the **top 4 reasons** why companies use skill assessment test to evaluate their **existing** employees:

Using-skill-assessment-test-to-evalute-employees

### Reason #1: To pinpoint training needs

By using skills assessment tests to evaluate their existing employees, employers can determine if there are some **important** job skills that their employees **l** **ack or should improve.**

Skill assessment test gives employers **precise data** on the skills that should be **developed** among their employees.

As a result, companies can organize **relevant and useful, even personalized training** and workshops for their employees.

### Reason #2: To identify best performers

Skill assessment tests are a great way to **identify your best performers** and **reward** them accordingly. These tests provide an **objective and transparent** method for evaluating your employees.

Consequently, you can **reward** your top performers with a bonus, job promotion, raise and/or social recognition. That way, you’ll ensure their **motivation** **and loyalty.**

### Reason #3: To identify potential leaders

Another great benefit of using a skill assessment test is their ability to **identify potential leaders**. There are **specialized** skill assessment tests which evaluate and measure your employees **leadership capabilities, skills and knowledge.**

That way, you can put the **right** type of people on your **key** positions. Those people with a **real** potential to lead others. Maybe you **already** have such talent among your employees – and you don’t **even** know it!

### Reason #4: To benchmark your talent

Finally, skill assessment tests are used to **compare** your employees against **industry** or **geographical** benchmarks.

**Comparing** the talent that you have within your company with your competitors is **crucial** for your company’s **success**.

You can’t move as fast as your competitors if you don’t have just as **talented** employees as they do!

## Conclusion: The benefits of using a skills assessment test

Utilizing skill assessment tests helps companies **hire the right people** for their open positions. These tests are also useful for **managing your existing employees** and placing them in the right positions.

As a result, companies can avoid the **high cost of bad hires** and **ensure higher productivity** of their employees, which positively affects the **company’s bottom line**.

In short, using skill assessment tests helps companies make **well informed decisions** when it comes to **hiring, training and promoting** their job candidates and employees.

## How to implement skill assessment tests?

Now that you’ve discovered all the **benefits** of skill assessment tests, it’s time to **start using** them in your hiring process.
